title: Drowned
content:
혻혻혻혻[Footnote: In the Grand River, at Brantford, July 30th, 1875, Miss Jessie Hamilton, adopted daughter of C.H. Waterous, Esq., Brantford, aged 14 years and 3 months, and Miss Ella E. Murton, only daughter of John W. Murton, Esq., Hamilton, aged 14 years.]


혻혻혻혻혻혻혻혻The morning dawned without a cloud,
혻혻혻혻But evening came with pall and shroud, - 
혻혻혻혻With muffled step, and bated breath,
혻혻혻혻And mournful whisperings of - death!

혻혻혻혻혻혻혻혻혻혻혻혻혻혻혻혻 *혻혻혻혻혻혻혻혻혻혻혻혻 *혻혻혻혻혻혻혻혻혻혻혻혻 *

혻혻혻혻혻혻혻혻Young lips, that in the morning sung
혻혻혻혻The summer's opening flowers among,
혻혻혻혻Were hushed and cold; - young, laughing eyes,
혻혻혻혻That met the dawn with sweet surprise,
혻혻혻혻Were darkly sealed; - young feet, that pressed
혻혻혻혻The dewy turf with glad unrest,
혻혻혻혻Were cold and stirless, never more
혻혻혻혻To tread the paths they trod before; - 
혻혻혻혻And they, who in the morning strayed
혻혻혻혻In fawn-like freedom down the glade,
혻혻혻혻In solemn, dreamless slumber lay,
혻혻혻혻To wake no more, at fall of day!

혻혻혻혻혻혻혻혻O stern, remorseless, sullen Tide!
혻혻혻혻O dark Flood, never satisfied!
혻혻혻혻Couldst thou not pity, when, to thee
혻혻혻혻Those young lambs sped so trustingly?
혻혻혻혻Nay, nay; - the tempest's stormy wrath
혻혻혻혻Spares not the lily in its path! - 
혻혻혻혻The tameless river will not rest,
혻혻혻혻To heed the rose-leaf on its breast! - 
혻혻혻혻A moment, and the quiet shore
혻혻혻혻Heard a low wail, and heard no more; - 
혻혻혻혻And then, with calm, unaltered mien,
혻혻혻혻The river glided on serene - 
혻혻혻혻With what a weight of anguish fraught! - 
혻혻혻혻Unconscious of the woe it wrought.

혻혻혻혻혻혻혻혻"Dust unto dust!" O God, thy way
혻혻혻혻Strange and mysterious seems to-day,
혻혻혻혻As, in the darkness of the tomb,
혻혻혻혻What but an hour ago was bloom
혻혻혻혻And beauty, now we hide away,
혻혻혻혻And leave to silence and decay!
혻혻혻혻Aid us in lowliness to bow,
혻혻혻혻And own how just and good art thou,
혻혻혻혻And, though thou hidest still thy face,
혻혻혻혻Trust the great love we may not trace!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Pamela S. Vining, (J. C. Yule), titled "Drowned", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
